title: Tomescu's Conjecture
statement_text: For every connected graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with chromatic number $k \geq 4$, $$P_G(k) \leq k!(k-1)^{n-k},$$ with equality if and only if the 2-core of $G$ is a $k$-clique (i.e., $G$ is formed by attaching a tree to each vertex of the complete graph $K_k$).
context_text: Tomescu posed this upper bound on the chromatic polynomial in 1971. The inequality is easily verified when $G$ contains a $k$-clique, and Tomescu further conjectured that equality characterises exactly those graphs whose 2-core is a $k$-clique. The condition $k \geq 4$ is necessary: for $k=2$ equality holds for all connected bipartite graphs, while for $k=3$ the bound fails for odd cycles of length at least five.
paper_contribution: Proves the full conjecture for all $k \geq 4$ and establishes the equality characterisation, completing the proof after Knox and Mohar had previously settled the cases $k=4$ and $k=5$.
paper_title: A proof of Tomescu's graph coloring conjecture
